qText: A certain amount increases to 3 times its initial value after 10 years of compounding interest. How many years will it take for it to grow to twenty-seven times its original amount?

A person invests ‚āĻ1,20,000 at 6% p.a. compounded annually for 2 years. He then reinvests the amount for 2 more years at 8% p.a. compounded annually. What is the total compound interest earned at the end of 4 years?

The compound interest on ‚āĻ10,000 at 20% per annum is ‚āĻ4,641. If compounded half yearly, for how many years was the principal invested ?

Jonathan had borrowed a sum of money 3 years ago at 10% interest per annum compounded annually for a 5-year period, with the amount to be paid at the end of the period being ‚āĻ1,61,051. However, there is no prepayment penalty and Jonathan has received some bonus payment now, with which he has decided to clear his debt. How much does Jonathan have to pay now to clear his debt ?
Money is doubled in a bank account in 7 years, when the Interest is compounded annually: What time in years is needed to make an amount 8 times in the bank ?

A deposited ‚āĻ1,56,250 at 8% simple interest for 2 years. How much more money will A have in his account at the end of two years, if at the same rate of interest the sum is invested in compound interest, compounded annually.

A certain amount of money was lent for a period of 1 year 9 months at a rate of 10% per annum compounded annually. If the compound interest is ‚āĻ1,460, find the amount of money lent.

Rs. 2400 is lent at the rate of 10 percent per annum. Which of the following statement(s) is/are correct? I. The compound interest (compounding annually) for 3 years is Rs. 794.4. II. The difference between simple interest and compound interest (compounding annually) after 3 years is Rs. 75.8
optText: ‚Äč(a) None of the statements are correct‚Äč
‚Äč(d) Both statement I and II are correct‚Äč

qText: A sum of Rs.7500 amounts to Rs.15000 in two years at compound interest calculated annually. How much will Rs.7500 becomes in four years?

A sum was lent for one year at the rate of 16 percent per annum on compound interest (compounding annually). If the compounding had been done half yearly, then the interest would have increased by Rs. 64. What was the sum lent?

What is the effective annual rate of interest corresponding to a nominal rate of 10% per annum payable half - yearly?

Rs. 6000 is lent at the annual rate of 10 percent for 2 years. Which of the following is statement(s) is/are correct ? I. Simple interest to be received on this sum is Rs. 1200 II. Compound Interest (compounding annually) to be received on this sum is Rs. 1260
optText: ‚Äč(b) Both statement I and II are correct‚Äč

qText: Vipul and Manish invested the sum of 15000 and 20000 at the rate of 20 ‚āĻ ‚āĻ percent p.a. and 30 percent p.a. respectively on compound interest (compounding annually). If the time period is 3 years for both, then what will be the total compound interest earned by Vipul and Manish ?

A person invests Rs 55,000 for two years on compound interest such that the rate of interest for the first year is 8% per annum and that for the second year is 12% per annum, and the interest is compounded annually. What is the total interest earned by the person ?

A borrows an amount of ‚āĻ3,600 from B at a rate of 20% simple interest for 2 years and lends 75% of this amount to C at 20% compound interest for 2 years. A uses the remaining amount for his personal purpose. Find the total loss incurred by A.

A certain sum is invested at 26% p.a, interest compounded annually. In how many years (approx) will it double ?

Divide ‚āĻ66,300 between A and B in such a way that the amount that A receives after 8 years is equal to the amount that B receives after 10 years; with compound interest being compounded annually at a rate of 10% per annum.

What was the overall interest rate for three years ?
